5a8d5d9e549b9d585c69b00acbc935f3a9f6ed01: Gold doesn't make a profit, why do so many people worship it?
879d3aaf1f8bef1ec72ac32e8cda679deb047a71: Experts believe that the root of the "cult" of gold does not only come from hoarding psychology but reflects limitations in the development of the financial system. Gold is considered an asset that does not add value to the economy, so cash flow into gold can reduce the efficiency of capital allocation. The central solution is to improve investor confidence in the financial market instead of relying solely on administrative measures. This implies the need to improve the quality of investment products, market transparency and policy stability to attract capital away from gold. Positive indirect impact for the stock market and banks if financial confidence improves, but the article does not mention specific stocks for individual evaluation.
dcb8fee332817e5f2635d17a2ca2f2b2fb400a8f: The world's largest fund continues to "discharge" tons of gold

World gold prices increased 1.7% in the last session of April and regained the mark of 4,600 USD/oz, after falling nearly 4,500 USD/oz on April 29. Short-term motivation comes from the weakening USD and oil prices, making gold more attractive to buyers outside the US. TD Securities believes that gold remains under pressure from inflation risks, high real interest rates and weak institutional demand, but maintains a positive long-term forecast with year-end prices above 5,000 USD/oz.

36530915d191495cd8906c973edafe96b7c1997c: “Birds are afraid of curved branches”: The invisible resistance of the stock market
66bf8dce2b48b88fe7962ca8f54f3459126d2ae4: The article emphasizes that the "birds are afraid of bent branches" mentality is the major drag on the stock market, when geopolitical or policy shocks often only act as triggers for sell-offs. In March, VN-Index decreased in 14/21 sessions, on March 9 alone it lost more than 115 points, equivalent to 6.51%, in the context of Brent oil rising to 111 USD/barrel. A similar shock was the April 3 session, when the VN-Index dropped nearly 88 points, equivalent to 6.68%, after news that the US imposed a 46% reciprocal tax on Vietnam, then recovered when there was news of a 90-day tax delay. With individual investors accounting for more than 80% of transactions, the market easily falls into a cycle of sell-offs, technical pullbacks, FOMO and then losses, causing many people to buy at the top and sell at the bottom. The overall impact is negative in the short term on market sentiment and liquidity, but there are no specific stocks in the article to evaluate separately.

1cd854ccaae412c5c2affcddff938dd946bcd80b: Exploiting intellectual property rights to contribute capital, mortgage loans, what do businesses need to pay attention to?
bfe9f95fc9cd1c94f9bf65bccaa4ea88c7991fac: The Law amending and supplementing the Intellectual Property Law was issued on December 10, 2025 and effective from April 1, 2026, officially encouraging the use of intellectual property rights to contribute capital or mortgage loans. The new regulations supplement Article 8a, requiring owners to create a separate management list if intellectual property assets do not meet the conditions for recording in accounting books, and at the same time allow exploitation in civil, commercial, and investment transactions. According to the Enterprise Law 2020, amended in 2025, intellectual property rights are considered capital assets if they can be valued in Vietnam Dong and the capital contributor must be the legal owner or have legal use rights. For assets with registered ownership, capital contributors must carry out procedures to transfer rights to the company, helping to limit the risk of using the same invention, trademark or software to contribute capital in many places. Credit growth needs to be flexible, prioritize production and business and growth drivers, while strictly controlling credit in risky areas, especially researching real estate classification to apply appropriate limits and encourage social housing and industrial parks. Regarding the gold market, the Government requires the implementation of Notice 211, limiting the psychology of hoarding/speculation so that cash flow can return to production, business or bank deposits. Bond crises often lead to spikes in yields , dwindling liquidity and can force central bank intervention, as in the case of 2022 UK Government bonds . Dimon assessed that the private credit market of about 1,700 billion USD is not large enough to pose a systemic risk, but warned that the credit recession after a long period of quiet could be more serious than expected. Short-term negative information for international financial/banking groups such as JPMorgan Chase if yields fluctuate strongly and credit risks increase, although there is no direct quantitative data on the profit impact.

01dd99ccec5573a78e7cf87179702511b0f93344: SBV proposes to standardize system safety according to Basel III
aefad1fc7c710df8f7a83c1846df9749d440a223: The State Bank of Vietnam proposes to standardize system safety according to Basel III. The State Bank (SBV) is collecting public comments on the draft Circular stipulating limits and safety ratios in the operations of banks and foreign bank branches. This is considered an important step to replace Circular 22/2019/TT-NHNN, aiming to apply more stringent international standards to protect the system against liquidity and leverage risks. Approaching Basel III standards with new ratios The most important highlight in this draft is the addition of liquidity and capital management ratios according to Basel III standards. Specifically: Solvency ratio (LCR): The draft introduces the LCR ratio to ensure banks have enough high liquid assets to meet payment needs in a 30-day stress scenario. Net stable resources ratio (NSFR): Replaces the maximum ratio of short-term capital for medium and long-term loans

Strategic investors must be selected by the General Meeting of Shareholders, have financial/technological capacity and commit to cooperation for at least 03 years; Professional investors must meet financial or professional capacity conditions according to the Securities Law. ed0196ad0c3e5fe5930cf0e7e0dc195cde9f9810: USD deposit interest rate is 0%, how do banks "retain" USD?
481ef677ae08be68e53148db21c2084561be33b4: Since 2016, Vietnam has maintained a 0% USD deposit interest rate to reduce dollarization, encourage holding VND and limit foreign currency speculation. Even though there is no nominal interest, customers still keep USD because of exchange rate expectations, forcing banks to "retain" foreign currency sources by pledging USD to borrow VND at an interest rate of only 1.8%/year, the limit is nearly equal to the value of USD and the term is usually less than 6 months. For banks, this model supports NIM thanks to the 0% USD capital cost, which can be exploited via swap or international payment loans with interest rates of about 3-5%, but still subject to risks when VND interest rates increase or USD depreciates. On March 13, DXY increased nearly 3% to 100.36 points, the highest in nearly 10 months, and VCBS said that the strong USD trend may continue due to geopolitical tensions, energy prices and expectations that the Fed will keep interest rates high. The impact is generally positive in the short term for the banking group with large USD resources thanks to increased service/foreign currency loan revenue

70a48cd6517675fe05bd0029f45796c567c2b9b5: Vice Chairman of the State Securities Commission: The General Meeting of Shareholders is no longer a procedure, but a "test of confidence" of businesses before investors
266192cefbf8375ce8943beb8808f62f5ec8c531: The annual General Meeting of Shareholders is emphasized as the core governance institution, where shareholders exercise their rights to know, ask and vote on key issues of the enterprise. Mr. Nguyen Hoang Duong said that 2026 is an important period for Vietnam's stock market to raise standards of structure, transparency, governance and approach international practices. Compared to 5–10 years ago, the quality of transparency at the General Meeting of Shareholders has clearly improved thanks to the requirement to send documents at least 21 days before the meeting date, increased explanations and expansion of online/hybrid formats. The remaining bottleneck is that the quality of the organization is not uniform, some businesses publish response information, shallow explanations and do not meet shareholder expectations. The overall impact is positive for the group of listed enterprises with good governance and high transparency because it helps strengthen investor confidence and attract medium and long-term capital, but negative for enterprises with weak transparency due to increased supervision pressure.

The market in April increased unsustainably, mainly thanks to the Vingroup group, while expectations of upgrading, lowering interest rates and first quarter business results were reflected but did not create a spillover. For the Vingroup group, the short-term impact is still positive thanks to expectations of infrastructure, real estate and consumption projects, but valuations are no longer cheap, so if profit is taken, it will have a strong negative impact on VN-Index. Experts recommend being cautious, waiting for a strong adjustment period to have a more attractive price range, prioritizing observing Banks, Consumption and Public Investment.

831a0b0c4d4973fe20c45100a700eae63526e68f: BMP Chairman: Raw material prices are unlikely to return to their old level when the war ends
b512201521ddeb40e1795fb03e3174e1993c83ea: BMP's 2026 Annual General Meeting of Shareholders on April 28 focused on the risk of rising raw material prices and selling price policy in the plastic pipe industry. Management said that BMP has increased selling prices accordingly as input costs escalated, and used automation and AI to control costs, helping production capacity to be among the industry's leading. Chairman Sakchai commented that even though the war ends, raw material prices will be difficult to return to old levels in the short term because many chemical factories need time to recover, so reducing selling prices will be a challenge. BMP prioritizes domestic raw materials, still imports when the price is reasonable and affirms a diverse PVC supply chain, not dependent on one market; SCG's suspension of a petrochemical complex is assessed to have no significant impact. The information is generally slightly positive for BMP because the Company is able to maintain profit margins through price adjustments/discounts, but competition is still high when Vietnam has nearly 200 coin manufacturers.

66fd68f3042af8096576d418f3f505ec01836137: BMP General Meeting of Shareholders: Confident in completing plans, cautious when participating in projects
e2536b298047b1219994f1a0cf8d7d1ddab6211a: BMP's 2026 Annual General Meeting of Shareholders on April 28 approved the revenue plan of 6,053 billion VND, PBT of 1,597 billion VND, PAT of 1,278 billion VND, an increase of 8% and 4% respectively compared to 2025. The Board of Directors is confident in completing the plan despite fluctuations in raw material prices, transportation and supply, thanks to output partnerships and stable supply. BMP plans to invest 279 billion VND to improve production quality, innovate technology, develop new products and expand production in the North, where capacity utilization is about 60%. The company is cautious with the project segment due to low profit margins and debt risks, and has selectively expanded into agriculture, selling plastic pipes for aquaculture and large-scale banana cultivation. 9d3806bfaa258048e64f77e3b1fe45610008fd9e: 7 key members in OPEC+ agree to increase oil exploitation output. The decision to increase oil exploitation output of 7 key members in OPEC+, similar to the increase of 206,000 barrels/day in April, was adjusted after the withdrawal of the UAE. Seven member countries of the Organization of Petroleum Exporting Countries and its partners (OPEC+) have agreed in principle to a plan to increase target oil production by about 188,000 barrels/day in June, signaling their determination to maintain production plans despite the United Arab Emirates (UAE) suddenly leaving the group on May 1. The above measure - expected to be introduced at an online policy meeting on May 3, appears in the context of geopolitical tensions that are seriously limiting the ability to achieve OPEC + goals.

The S&P 500 index has risen 27% over the past five years, to a record above 7,200 points, while Berkshire Hathaway shares have fallen 8% in the same period. In the past quarter, Berkshire sold a net $8 billion in shares, bringing cash to a record $380 billion at the end of March. Faced with a challenging market and an increasingly large amount of cash, Mr. Buffett said the company has the right team and is ready to "choose the right time to act."

a52e3d5c33d3c1d45516142122afd006daa84194: 2026 General Meeting of Shareholders Season: Digitalization and transparency gap The 2026 General Meeting of Shareholders season shows clear improvements in technology and organizational forms such as digitized documents, electronic voting, and online meetings. But behind that modern shell, a core problem still exists: the substantive dialogue rights of shareholders with the board of directors is still limited, causing the congress to not become a true governance institution. According to the provisions of the Enterprise Law 2020, public companies must hold an annual General Meeting of Shareholders (GMS) within 4 months from the end of the fiscal year, and can be extended up to 6 months if approved. For most businesses with a fiscal year ending on December 31, the deadline to hold a general meeting is usually April 29 or June 30 at the latest. Data from VietstockFinance shows that in the first 4 months of 2026, there were more than 950 listed and registered businesses.

However, the real estate market structure has not developed adequately.

2dc6c6ba2f17b6ea2bd29806126a3c08d27862ac: How to determine when to apply corporate income tax incentives for new investment projects
fd8d93eacb2350a7373229f7df16ab37a8c3e234: How to determine when to apply corporate income tax incentives for new investment projects In the tax legal policy system, corporate income tax (CIT) incentives are always considered one of the important tools to attract investment, promote economic restructuring and encourage the development of priority industries. The practice of applying tax law over many years shows that, although the regulations on corporate income tax incentives have been relatively fully regulated in the Law on Corporate Income Tax, Decree and guiding Circulars, there still exist many different interpretations related to when to start calculating the tax exemption and tax reduction period. Many businesses understand that incentives are calculated from the time the project comes into operation or from the first year of revenue generation. Meanwhile, some local tax authorities apply based on the time taxable income arises, leading to inconsistencies.

097042f393d4e0fffb3e04a75d31d330b34f4420: The State Bank recently announced the draft amendment to Circular 22/2019 regulating limits and safety ratios in the operations of banks and foreign bank branches. One of the notable changes in the draft is the change from the target "ratio of outstanding loans to total deposits" (LDR) to "ratio of outstanding credit loans to capital mobilization" (CDR). Compared to the previous approach, this target is determined on the basis of total outstanding credit and total capital mobilized according to regulations, instead of being limited to loans and deposits. The calculation components basically inherit current regulations, including corporate bond investments, off-balance sheet commitments on the credit granting side, as well as capital sources other than deposits on the mobilization side. Regarding deposits of the State Treasury, the draft has adjustments in the way it is calculated into the bank's mobilized capital.

According to Official Dispatch No. 3760/UBCK-QLKD dated May 7, the State Securities Commission (SSC) said that recently, many organizations and individuals have posted market analysis, technical analysis and recommendations related to buying, selling and holding stocks on social networks. 080478e92998d955770e86abe373f69d957d48ab: What is the asset-light model that VinFast has just restructured?
d55b9325147969c57471ee5c7b412a6607e33029: After many years of pursuing a model of comprehensive autonomy from production, assembly to sales, VinFast has just announced a notable strategic turning point: divesting the entire production segment in Vietnam with a transaction value of about 530 million USD and switching to an "asset-light" operating model - also known as the asset optimization model. According to filings with the US Securities Commission (SEC), the Vietnamese electric vehicle company will separate its production activities in Vietnam to another legal entity, while retaining assets related to technology, intellectual property, brand and sales network. After restructuring, the production part will operate in the form of contract manufacturing instead of being directly owned and operated by VinFast as before. This move quickly attracted the attention of the financial community and the automotive community because it shows that VinFast is significantly changing its approach in the context of the global electric vehicle industry entering a period of fierce competition.

The State Bank (SBV) recently announced a public consultation on the draft Circular replacing Circular 22/2019/TT-NHNN regulating limits and safety ratios in the operations of banks and foreign bank branches. The notable change in the draft is the change from the target "ratio of outstanding loans to total deposits" (LDR) to "ratio of outstanding credit loans to capital mobilization" (CDR). The draft also continues to maintain the ratio of outstanding credit balance to capital mobilization at a maximum of 85%, similar to current regulations. If the new calculation method according to the draft Circular is applied, the CDR rate of many banks may change significantly compared to the LDR rate. 4c7fd7e89edf752a52ee7fd02b5d1717e4839384: Impact of the new regulation on removing the taxable revenue threshold for business households
6bb209c44b21531fc75c7e30e5cc9d6b78c8c53c: Impact of the new regulation on removing the taxable revenue threshold for business households On April 24, 2026, the National Assembly passed the Law amending and supplementing a number of articles of the Law on Personal Income Tax, the Law on Value Added Tax, the Law on Corporate Income Tax and the Law on Special Consumption Tax, marking an important shift in tax policy for business households and individual businesses. A prominent content of this law is the abolition of the revenue threshold of 500 million VND/year as a basis for determining tax obligations for business households. According to the new regulations, the law no longer sets a specific taxable revenue level but shifts to a mechanism that assigns the Government to regulate tax-free revenue levels appropriate to each period of socio-economic development. This change shows that tax policy is shifting from a model of hard regulations in law to a flexible operating mechanism to ensure adaptability to economic fluctuations. Law

5a190f6038716106088179bf86b5c7aa2edffc18: Persist in the goal of converting E10 biofuel
c74b8a00446f5b3eb6954d020f04a8961043359c: Persevering with the goal of converting to E10 biofuel, Vietnam is catching up with the world's trend when deploying E10 biofuel, and can upgrade to E15 or E20 gasoline like many countries to increase energy independence, reduce environmental pollution and solve output problems for the agricultural industry. E10 gasoline began being sold on the market from the end of April. Around the world, the United States has used E10 gasoline since the 80s and is currently deploying E85. Meanwhile, the Philippines and Thailand are popularizing E15 gasoline and plan to upgrade it to E20 from 2026. In Vietnam, the roadmap for mandatory use of E10 gasoline nationwide will be applied from June 1, 2026. Many key enterprises even accelerated the implementation progress to reduce the pressure on importing petroleum in the context of prolonged Middle East tensions.
